title: THE EIGHTH PASSENGER — LIFELINE CUT V6 FINAL
episodeObjective: Reach the external emergency pod, identify the occupant, and make an irreversible decision about Noah Voss.

summary: Scenario spine preserved: pod discovery, scan/approach, hatch decision, Noah identification, irreversible extract/seal branch.
All long physical tasks use characterBusy + returnNodeId.
No visible wait duration is exposed to the player.
Maya reports what happened while away after each busy state.
Every visible choice set has exactly two choices.
Character dialogue rewritten for natural conversation and emotional continuity.
Hale and Maya conflict escalates through behavior, not UI trust text.
Lore is delivered through action, physical evidence and reaction.

e08_n01.speaker: MAYA
e08_n01.objective: Establish the pod as the concrete rescue target.

events.text: I found the pod.
The one the archive said would remember.
It's outside the broken relay spine, caught in the antenna frame like something tried to leave and got grabbed on the way out.
The beacon is blinking in a rhythm. Not random. Not dying.
Someone is tapping from the other side of a wall, and I hate that I know what hope sounds like now.
There. Tell me you heard that too.

choices.text: GUIDE MAYA CLOSER TO THE POD.

events.text: Good. Scan first.
I know I sound like I want to sprint at it, but that's mostly because my brain is terrible at grief.
I'm setting the relay pack against a support strut and routing the scan through the pod beacon.
I need to keep the antenna aligned. If I talk, I lose the return.
Scan complete.
I had to hold the relay steady against the strut the whole time. My shoulder is numb, but the data is clean.

events.title: POD MANIFEST
events.content:
Occupant: UNKNOWN
Life Support: 23%
Pressure: unstable but contained
Beacon Origin: external emergency pod
Visual Feed: unavailable
Manual Access: possible
Warning: identity not confirmed
Carrier Residue: 7.14 MHz harmonic
events.caption: Remote scan confirms an active life-support pod with an unknown occupant.

events.text: Unknown occupant. Critical status. Manual access possible.
That last part feels like the station putting a knife in my hand and calling it a tool.
Moving closer.
I'm clipping myself to the service rail. If I drift away, pretend this was heroic and not deeply stupid.
The gantry is exposed. Broken antenna ribs on the left. Open dark on the right.
I need both hands for the crossing.
I'm close enough to see frost on the hatch.
No visual through the port. Just my reflection looking like someone who has made several bad decisions in a row.
The pod corrected its own drift while I was crossing.
Not much. Just enough to prove it isn't passive wreckage.
There are two ways to reach the hatch.
Short path across the exposed antenna rib. Fast, ugly, probably haunted by physics.
Long path through the maintenance tether. Slower, less exposed, but the tether housing is damaged.
I would love a third option called wake up somewhere safe.
Fast route. Of course.
I'm stepping onto the rib. It is narrower than it looked from back there.
No talking while I cross. If I hear myself panic, I may start agreeing with it.
I'm at the hatch frame.
The rib flexed under me. I said a word I will not repeat because even the vacuum deserves dignity.
My tether line snapped tight once. If the rail had failed, we would be having a very different conversation.
But I'm here. Faster than the careful version of me would approve.
Tether path.
Sensible. I hate how much I appreciate sensible right now.
I'm checking handholds and suit line integrity as I go.
This will take longer. Don't make a speech while I'm gone. I won't hear it.
I'm at the hatch frame.
I found scratches inside the tether housing. Fresh enough that the dust hasn't settled in them.
Something else used this route. Or someone dragged something through it.
Good news: my line is intact. Bad news: my imagination is not.
I'm at the hatch.
The pod is vibrating. Tiny pulses through the frame.
Not enough to move it. Enough to feel like it has a heartbeat.
Hale just went very quiet. That's never good.
Hale?
Step back from it.
events.speaker: HALE

events.text: There it is. The calm voice.
events.speaker: MAYA

events.text: Maya, please. That pod should not be here.
events.speaker: HALE

events.text: Thank you.
events.speaker: HALE

events.text: I know how I sound. I know I sound like a man making fear wear a lab coat.
events.speaker: HALE

events.text: But if the pod is correcting its own drift without visible guidance, it is not passive wreckage.
events.speaker: HALE

events.text: Maya, give me thirty seconds.
events.speaker: HALE

events.text: You get twenty.
events.speaker: MAYA

events.text: I'm running the hatch integrity check from here.
events.speaker: HALE

events.text: Check complete.
events.speaker: HALE

events.title: POD HATCH INTEGRITY
events.content:
Seal: intact
External frost: fresh
Manual lever: responsive
Motion correction: active
Carrier residue: 7.14 MHz harmonic
Risk: opening may trigger automatic occupant revival sequence
events.caption: Hatch check shows active 7.14 residue and a possible automatic revival sequence.
events.message: [POD HATCH INTEGRITY ARCHIVED TO DATA BANK]

events.text: Automatic revival sequence.
events.speaker: MAYA

events.text: That's a phrase that should require a permit.
events.speaker: MAYA

events.text: I'm putting my hand on the hatch.
It's colder than it should be. Even through the glove.
No. No, don't say my name.
The hatch display woke up when I touched it.

events.title: POD CONTACT WAKE
events.content:
Manual contact detected
Display state: partial wake
Suit registry ping returned
Recovered ID fragment: NV-08
Warning: occupant identity unresolved
events.caption: Manual contact wakes the pod display and returns suit fragment NV-08.
events.message: [POD CONTACT WAKE ARCHIVED TO DATA BANK]

events.text: NV-08. Noah's mark.
Tell me this isn't what I think it is.
We have three facts.
The manifest says unknown occupant.
The suit fragment says NV-08.
The life signs say someone is alive.
I don't need a matching module to understand why that scares me.
Noah Voss used NV-08 on everything.
Tool tags. Cable caps. Ration boxes.
He said being prepared was just anxiety with stationery.
I miss that idiot.
Pulling the record.
events.speaker: HALE

events.title: DEATH RECORD: NOAH VOSS
events.content:
Subject: Noah Voss
Status: deceased
Reported Date: before Incident Alpha
Body Recovery: confirmed by Engineering Lead
Witness: Ethan Hale
Conflict: active biometric signal currently transmitting under suit ID NV-08
events.caption: Recovered death record directly conflicts with pod telemetry.
events.message: [DEATH RECORD: NOAH VOSS ARCHIVED TO DATA BANK]

events.text: No. Voss is dead.
events.speaker: HALE

events.text: I saw the body myself.
events.speaker: HALE

events.text: Frost on the visor. Suit seal locked. His hand still curled like he was about to argue with someone.
events.speaker: HALE

events.text: He usually was.
events.speaker: MAYA

events.text: He was a bad engineer.
events.speaker: HALE

events.text: He was a good person.
events.speaker: HALE

events.text: Most days I needed the second one more.
events.speaker: HALE

events.text: I'm putting the mic against the hull.
No one breathe dramatically. That includes me.
I need silence for this. Real silence.

events.speaker: UNKNOWN
events.text: not suit

events.speaker: UNKNOWN
events.text: not dead
That was not mechanical.
Maya, step back.
events.speaker: HALE

events.text: You keep saying that like stepping back has ever saved anyone here.
events.speaker: MAYA

events.text: Life support is dropping.
Twenty-three became twenty-one while we were arguing.
If we delay too long, whoever is inside dies for real. Or dies again. I hate this sentence.
If we open it wrong, the pod could depressurize or wake up whatever signal is riding it.
events.speaker: HALE

events.text: I don't want plans. I want a decision.
events.speaker: MAYA

events.text: Fast rescue means I open the hatch and pull the occupant line. Controlled breach means we clamp first and risk the clock.
events.speaker: MAYA

choices.text: FAST RESCUE: OPEN THE HATCH NOW.

events.text: Fast rescue.
Good. Terrible. Good.
I'm releasing the manual latch and pulling the occupant line.
Hatch released.
Pressure kicked back. My shoulder hit the frame.
I'm fine.
That was too quick. She could have lost the seal.
events.speaker: HALE

events.text: But I didn't.
events.speaker: MAYA

events.text: And someone inside just moved.
Controlled breach.
events.speaker: HALE

events.text: Thank you for choosing the option least likely to turn Maya into a cautionary diagram.
events.speaker: HALE

events.text: I heard that.
events.speaker: MAYA

events.text: I'm anchoring the hatch while he calculates pressure release.
events.speaker: MAYA

events.text: Seal clamped.
My hands are shaking. Not from fear. From cold. Mostly.
You can admit fear.
events.speaker: HALE

events.text: You first.
events.speaker: MAYA

events.text: Absolutely not.
events.speaker: HALE

events.text: Opening now.
The hatch is open.
There's frost everywhere.
I see a suit.
NV-08 on the chest.
Helmet is cracked. Not enough to kill him if the pod kept pressure, but enough to make the universe feel personal.
The faceplate is fogged from the inside.
Inside means breathing.
Inside means I have to look.
Clearing the faceplate.
No.
No, no, no.
It looks like Noah.
Maya, step away from the pod.
events.speaker: HALE

events.text: Don't you dare make this smaller than it is.
events.speaker: MAYA

events.text: His eyes are moving under the lids.

events.speaker: UNKNOWN
events.text: where
He heard that.
Checking wrist biometrics first.
Thank you for making me do the thing that doesn't involve staring at a possibly dead friend's face.

events.title: POD BIOMETRIC WRIST TRACE
events.content:
Suit ID: NV-08
Heartbeat: active irregular
Neural response: suppressed
Body temperature: survivable
Identity confidence: 61% Noah Voss
Warning: memory imprint contamination detected
events.caption: Wrist biometrics suggest Noah Voss but warn of memory imprint contamination.
events.message: [POD BIOMETRIC WRIST TRACE ARCHIVED TO DATA BANK]

events.text: Identity confidence sixty-one percent.
That's not enough to celebrate. It is absolutely enough to ruin me.
Memory imprint contamination?
events.speaker: HALE

events.text: There it is. The phrase that makes everything worse.
events.speaker: MAYA

events.text: Listen carefully.
events.speaker: HALE

events.text: If you disconnect him from the pod, whatever is sustaining the signal may come with him.
events.speaker: HALE

events.text: If you leave him connected, the pod may stabilize him but keep broadcasting to 7.14.
events.speaker: HALE

events.text: Translation?
events.speaker: MAYA

events.text: Save the body and risk bringing the signal in. Seal the pod and keep him alive but trapped.
events.speaker: HALE

events.text: Operator.
events.speaker: MAYA

events.text: I need you to make the call before I make the emotional one.
events.speaker: MAYA

choices.text: SEAL POD AND KEEP HIM ALIVE.

events.text: Extracting him.
Noah, if that's you, I'm going to be furious when you wake up.
I'm disconnecting the pod umbilicals and pulling him from the cradle.
He's out.
He's breathing.
He just grabbed my wrist.
Not hard. Like he was checking if I was real.

events.speaker: UNKNOWN
events.text: open
The pod just went dark behind us.
Maya, move. Now.
events.speaker: HALE

events.text: Seal the pod.
I hate you a little for making the smart call.
Not forever. Maybe not even for an hour.
But right now? A little.
I'm rerouting reserve oxygen and locking the 7.14 bleed channel.
Life support is stabilizing.
He is alive. He is trapped. Those are both true.
The pod still taps against the frame when the pressure cycles.
Sometimes survival is just a kinder word for delay.
events.speaker: HALE

events.text: Don't philosophize at me right now.
events.speaker: MAYA

events.text: I'm hauling him back through the tether.
He is heavier than he looks. Or I am more tired than I want to admit.
I need both hands. I'm moving now.
We're back inside.
His hand is still around my sleeve.
Noah?
He opened his eyes.
He said my name like he was afraid it belonged to someone else.

choices.text: LET NOAH REST AND MONITOR HIM.

events.text: I'm backing away from the pod.
I keep expecting him to knock from inside.
There. He did.
One tap. Then two.
Noah used to do that on bulkheads when he wanted attention.
Or something in there knows that.
I need to get out of the pod frame before I change my mind.
I'm clear of the pod frame.
The beacon is steadier now. That should make me feel better.
It doesn't.
I can still hear the tapping in the tether line. Maybe it is vibration. Maybe I am lying to myself.
Keeping him awake.
Noah. Stay with me. Look at my hand if you can't look at my face.
He's trying to speak.

events.speaker: UNKNOWN
events.text: where is eva
He said Eva.
Hale just stopped breathing on the channel.
I don't breathe.
events.speaker: HALE

events.text: You know what I mean.
events.speaker: MAYA

events.text: Letting him rest.
I hate that the right thing can look so much like abandoning someone.
His pulse is ugly but present.
He said one thing before his eyes closed again.

events.speaker: UNKNOWN
events.text: eva
Hale, why does that name make the signal colder?
We sealed him in.
I keep repeating that because maybe if I say it enough, it becomes a strategy instead of a wound.
The pod is alive. Noah is alive. The channel is not quiet.
There. That name again.
Hale?
We need to find Eva Thorne.
events.speaker: HALE
UNKNOWN
events.text: EVA THORNE

events.speaker: UNKNOWN
events.text: SHE WAS THERE
Operator, Noah is alive.
events.speaker: MAYA

events.text: And I think the station just told us who it erased before him.
events.speaker: MAYA

choices.text: MOVE NOAH TO A SAFE ZONE.

events.speaker: UNKNOWN
events.text: EVA THORNE

events.speaker: UNKNOWN
events.text: SHE WAS THERE
Operator, Noah is still in the pod.
events.speaker: MAYA

events.text: And whatever is speaking through it just gave us a name.
events.speaker: MAYA

choices.text: LEAVE THE POD AND FIND EVA.

directorCut.coreObjective: Reach and identify the external emergency pod occupant, then make a permanent decision about Noah Voss.
majorChanges: All progressTask sequences were replaced with characterBusy events.
Every characterBusy has a returnNodeId and a full return report.
Maya's physical movement is separated from her reporting.
Noah reveal is more emotional, physical and consequential.
Hale and Maya conflict escalates naturally through dialogue.
Seal/extract branch creates different final states for Episode 09.
Eva Thorne is foreshadowed at the end of both branches.
All visible choices contain exactly two options.
